title: GPT-5.5 vs Claude: I Switched For 7 Days

content.text: People treat AI subscriptions kind of
like gym memberships. They think that
you have to pick one, stick with it
because switching feels like a huge
hassle, but it's really not. We ran
OpenAI's new GPT 5.5 model inside Codeex
and compared it against Clawude Code for
a full week of real business work. And
the truth is, they're both good, but at
different things. It's almost like a
leftrain, right brain kind of thing
that's going on. And I don't want you to
panic switch though. So this is a very
realistic video explaining not just
which one is better in which case but
how to think about the very concept of
choosing an AI model for your work. I'm
Mark Webster and I'm joined today by my
co-founder Gail Bratton at Authority
Hacker where we test all of this AI
stuff inside our business so business
owners like you don't have to. So Gail,
GPT 5.5 was released just last week.
They really seem to be pushing not just
the coding, which they seem to be
getting better at these days, but things
like research, data analysis, and
documents in a lot of their marketing
material for this. So, you've been
hands-on, you've tested it. You've
compared it to Claude Opus. What's your
initial take?

swapping completely for almost a week
now. And I think that's what makes it a

probably already seen the benchmarks and
all of that and it's not so interesting.

just want to say for some tasks I'm
going to default to it, but I'm not
going to default to it for everything.
The high level TLDDR is it's more

build these advanced skills with like
